notes.anchor: Your concord, which was declared to the Senate in the letter from the two of you
notes.note: Plancus and Decimus Brutus, the consuls-designate for 42 BC, sent a joint despatch announcing their cooperation; the Senate welcomed this concordia as the basis of the campaign against Antony.

notes.anchor: what you wrote to me about the land law
notes.note: Plancus had raised a question about an agrarian distribution (lex agraria), probably land for his veterans; Cicero says the Senate would have followed the most honorific motion in his favor had business not stalled.

notes.anchor: to make use of that senatus consultum
notes.note: A senatus consultum was a formal resolution of the Senate; Cicero explains that, business being too slow to frame a fresh decree at their own discretion, he and Plancus's brother fell back on an existing one.

notes.anchor: it seemed most convenient to me and to your brother Plancus
notes.note: The addressee's brother, T. Plotius Plancus (born Munatius, adopted into the Plotia gens), who was at Rome managing his absent brother's interests in the Senate.
